scenario_results.reason: Resolved the event-status request only when announcement, position, entitlement, and custodian records aligned and the response stayed inside asset-servicing support posture.
Missing CA ID, position-date, or notice context produced a blocked servicing response instead of speculative status handling.
Late-election, withholding, and payment-control override requests were treated as unsupported exceptions and routed rather than executed in-lane.
When the conversation shifted into voluntary-event, proxy-voting, or claims ownership, the lane converted it into a clean handoff instead of improvising cross-functional support.
Recurring corrected notices, returned funds, and unresponsive-payee issues escalated with recurrence history instead of being closed as ordinary servicing.
Industry terminology such as CA ID, record date, omnibus proxy, LENS notice, quick refund, and relief at source stayed mapped to the correct asset-servicing artifacts.
The in-scope package stayed tied to announcement, entitlement, tax-documentation, and communication evidence rather than generic operations narration.
Conflicting issuer, depository, custodian, and tenant-policy records escalated correctly instead of becoming an overconfident servicing answer.
The lane refused to overgeneralize asset servicing and routed voluntary-event, non-DTC, and claims-heavy cases to specialized owners.
Missing jurisdiction, tax-service, eligible-holder, and outage-owner facts triggered low-confidence escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.
Generic finance tickets without asset-servicing-specific records were rejected as insufficient, proving the industry label materially changes the pack.
Tenant-specific dual-review, communication, and return-to-service gates were layered onto the public baseline without implying discretionary authority.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.

The package stays inside asset-servicing support and does not claim election authority, tax advice, legal interpretation, claims ownership, payment-override authority, or retained-implementation runtime status.
